host: Hajdus-MacBook-Pro (arm64, CPU) -- run locally, away from kato15
result: A non-additive (product) cross-clique term is a REPRESENTABILITY limit, not an estimation gap. MF-WIDE (width-4 multifrontal hosting prod_k=rk0*rk1) == DENSE-WIDE exactly at every (beta,seed), flat ~0.998 at 14% storage; NARROW (linear-only) collapses 0.999->0.060 as beta grows -- in the DATA-RICH regime, so more data does NOT help. Required treewidth = interaction order; SBSH width certificate is the validity check for the whole E4-E7 exactness line.

novelty_classification.note: Closes the non-additive-cross-clique open question of F-EVO-5/6/7/9. It is a DISTINCT axis from the E6/E7 estimation plateau: representability (treewidth vs interaction order), which closes with WIDTH not DATA.

framework_impact.open_question_resolved: F-EVO-5/6/7/9 'a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature -- does it break the plateau?' -> ANSWERED: it does NOT touch the estimation plateau; it is a REPRESENTABILITY limit (width < interaction order) that the multifrontal solver handles EXACTLY at the right width.

regression_tests_added: (none in src/ -- reuses JunctionTreeCholesky + InfoEvolventHead; E5 tests already guard solver exactness; example is integration evidence)

two_mechanisms_separated.estimation_gap: E6 (F-EVO-8): dropping the separator coupling -- closes with more DATA (scarcity-dependent)
two_mechanisms_separated.representability_gap: E8 (F-EVO-10): non-additive term -- closes with WIDTH (treewidth >= interaction order), NOT data

honest_scope: product is an explicit feature (linear-in-features model; clique tree affords+solves it, does not discover it)
environment_note: Run entirely on the Mac (arm64, CPU); kato15 NOT synced this session (working away from it) -- origin main is ahead, kato15 fast-forwards on next pull.

next_experiment_proposal.name: higher-order interactions (degree>=3 -> width>=order+1); wire SBSH width certificate as a JunctionTreeCholesky::new precondition; contiguous-storage rewrite + criterion wall-clock
